aiken/interval/IntervalBound.title: IntervalBound
aiken/interval/IntervalBound.description: An interval bound, either inclusive or exclusive.

aiken/interval/IntervalBoundType.title: IntervalBoundType
aiken/interval/IntervalBoundType.description:
A type of interval bound. The value for the `Finite` case typically
 represents a number of seconds or milliseconds.

cardano/address/Address.title: Address
cardano/address/Address.description:
A Cardano `Address` typically holding one or two credential references.

 Note that legacy bootstrap addresses (a.k.a. 'Byron addresses') are
 completely excluded from Plutus contexts. Thus, from an on-chain
 perspective only exists addresses of type 00, 01, ..., 07 as detailed
 in [CIP-0019 :: Shelley Addresses](https://github.com/cardano-foundation/CIPs/tree/master/CIP-0019/#shelley-addresses).

cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/address/PaymentCredential.title: PaymentCredential
cardano/address/PaymentCredential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/address/StakeCredential.title: StakeCredential
cardano/address/StakeCredential.description:
Represent a type of object that can be represented either inline (by hash)
 or via a reference (i.e. a pointer to an on-chain location).

 This is mainly use for capturing pointers to a stake credential
 registration certificate in the case of so-called pointer addresses.

cardano/certificate/Certificate.title: Certificate
cardano/certificate/Certificate.description:
An on-chain certificate attesting of some operation. Publishing
 certificates triggers different kind of rules; most of the time,
 they require signatures from specific keys.

anyOf.title: RegisterCredential
anyOf.description:
Register a stake credential with an optional deposit amount.
 The deposit is always present when using the new registration certificate
 format available since the Conway era.

fields.title: deposit
fields.description:
> [!NOTE]
 > The `deposit` ought to be an `Option<Lovelace>`, but due to unfortunate
 > circumstances it will always be instantiated to `None` even when set in
 > the host transaction. This is what the `Never` type captures here.

anyOf.title: UnregisterCredential
anyOf.description:
Un-Register a stake credential with an optional refund amount
 The deposit is always present when using the new de-registration certificate
 format available since the Conway era.

fields.title: refund
fields.description:
> [!NOTE]
 > The `refund` ought to be an `Option<Lovelace>`, but due to unfortunate
 > circumstances it will always be instantiated to `None` even when set in
 > the host transaction. This is what the `Never` type captures here.

anyOf.title: RegisterAndDelegateCredential
anyOf.description: Register and delegate staking credential to a Delegatee in one certificate.

anyOf.title: RegisterDelegateRepresentative
anyOf.description:
Register a delegate representative (a.k.a DRep). The deposit is explicit and
 is refunded when the delegate steps down (unregister).

anyOf.title: UpdateDelegateRepresentative
anyOf.description:
Update a delegate representative (a.k.a DRep). The certificate also contains
 metadata which aren't visible on-chain.

anyOf.title: UnregisterDelegateRepresentative
anyOf.description: UnRegister a delegate representative, and refund back its past deposit.

fields.title: stake_pool
fields.description: The hash digest of the stake pool's cold (public) key

fields.title: vrf
fields.description: The hash digest of the stake pool's VRF (public) key

anyOf.title: RetireStakePool
anyOf.description: Retire a stake pool. 'at_epoch' indicates in which the retirement will take place

anyOf.title: AuthorizeConstitutionalCommitteeProxy
anyOf.description: Authorize a Hot credential for a specific Committee member's cold credential

anyOf.title: RetireFromConstitutionalCommittee
anyOf.description: Step down from the constitutional committee as a member.

cardano/certificate/Delegate.title: Delegate
cardano/certificate/Delegate.description:
A type of stake delegation that can be either block-production, vote or
 both. Note that delegation types aren't cancelling one another, so it is
 possible to delegate block production in one transaction, and delegate vote
 in another. This second delegation **does NOT** invalidate the first one.

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output

cardano/transaction/ValidityRange.title: ValidityRange

governance_voting/SimpleVoteRedeemer.title: SimpleVoteRedeemer
governance_voting/SimpleVoteRedeemer.description: Simple vote redeemer for always-yes validator

governance_voting/VoteRedeemer.title: VoteRedeemer
governance_voting/VoteRedeemer.description:
Redeemer for vote purpose - just contains participating signers
 Config is read from the single reference input

fields.title: participating_signers
fields.description: List of signers participating in this vote (for quorum validation)

mint_multi_validator/CounterDatum.title: CounterDatum
mint_multi_validator/CounterDatum.description: Datum stored in UTxO - a base counter value

mint_multi_validator/MintRedeemer.title: MintRedeemer
mint_multi_validator/MintRedeemer.description: Mint redeemer: list of (index, value) pairs for all script inputs

mint_multi_validator/SpendRedeemer.title: SpendRedeemer
mint_multi_validator/SpendRedeemer.description: Spend redeemer: must be datum.counter + own_index

multisig/MultiSig.title: MultiSig
multisig/MultiSig.description:
Recursive multisig policy type — mirrors the NativeScript structure.
 Used to verify the blueprint codegen correctly handles self-referential types.

anyOf.title: AnyOf
anyOf.description: Any one script must be satisfied (OR).

anyOf.title: AtLeast
anyOf.description: At least `required` scripts must be satisfied (M-of-N).

anyOf.title: Script
anyOf.description: A script hash that must be present.

stake_multivalidator/PublishRedeemer.title: PublishRedeemer
stake_multivalidator/PublishRedeemer.description:
Redeemer for publish (certificates): allows any action
 Could be extended to restrict certain operations

fields.title: placeholder
fields.description: Placeholder - certificates always allowed for this validator

stake_multivalidator/WithdrawRedeemer.title: WithdrawRedeemer
stake_multivalidator/WithdrawRedeemer.description: Redeemer for withdraw: list of input indices being coordinated
